bead.title: docs: reconcile stale read-coverage audit rows
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The 2026-04-29 read-coverage audit still lists many HTTP/MCP gaps even though its child read-coverage beads appear closed. Operators cannot tell whether the audit is complete, stale, or still an active backlog.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/02-design/read-coverage-audit-2026-04-29.md:30-106 lists missing surfaces across beads, docs, workers, exec runs, personas, metrics, and plugin reads.
- Tracker history shows the corresponding read-coverage child beads from ddx-44236615 are closed.
- Some closed read-coverage work may have had evidence-only or review-block history, so the audit should be spot-checked against current routes/tools before being marked superseded.

PROPOSED FIX
Run a current read-surface spot check for the audit rows, mark completed rows as implemented or superseded, and leave only real remaining gaps as a current backlog with new beads if needed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement missing endpoints in this bead unless the spot check finds a tiny doc-only mismatch. File separate implementation beads for real missing HTTP/MCP surfaces.
bead.acceptance:
1. docs/helix/02-design/read-coverage-audit-2026-04-29.md is marked current, completed, or superseded with a short reconciliation note.
2. Each G1-G12 row is spot-checked against current CLI/HTTP/MCP/GraphQL surfaces and classified as implemented, still missing, intentionally out-of-scope, or requiring a new bead.
3. Any real remaining missing read surface has a newly filed bead or an explicit reason it is deferred.
4. `rg -n "MISSING" docs/helix/02-design/read-coverage-audit-2026-04-29.md` only reports rows that are intentionally still active.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

metadata.work-last-detail: tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 1993005)
